channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Artemis II arrives in lunar space ahead of its trip around the Moon - Engadget
item.description:
1. Artemis II arrives in lunar space ahead of its trip around the Moon  Engadget

  4. Artemis II live updates: NASA's historic moon mission set to make lunar flyby today  Yahoo
  5. What time will Artemis II reach the moon? How to watch live  Cincinnati Enquirer

item.title: Astronomers thought the early universe was full of hydrogen: Now they've found it - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Astronomers thought the early universe was full of hydrogen: Now they've found it  Phys.org
  2. Astronomers Just Found 33,000 Hydrogen Halos the Early Universe Was Hiding  ScienceBlog.com

item.description: Scientists may have found a way to keep your bones strong for life  ScienceDaily

item.title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby - Space
item.description:
1. Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby  Space
  2. 58 Years After ‘Earthrise,’ NASA’s New Moonshot May Rediscover Earth  The New York Times
  3. Something special is happening in space right now  Big Think
  4. The Artemis II crew has a chance to recreate ‘Earthrise,’ one of the most famous photos of all time  National Geographic
  5. Earthrise 2.0 heralds dawn of the race to settle surface of the moon  observer.co.uk

item.title: Isaacman defends NASA budget proposal despite steep cuts - spacenews.com
item.description:

item.title: Artemis II toilet acts up again as astronauts speed toward the moon to break Apollo 13’s record - AL.com
item.description:
1. Artemis II toilet acts up again as astronauts speed toward the moon to break Apollo 13’s record  AL.com
  2. Artemis II astronauts have toilet trouble on their way towards the Moon  BBC
  3. Moon-bound Artemis II crew reflects on wonder of deep space experience: "You are special in all of this emptiness"  CBS News

  5. Artemis II Flight Update: Crew and Ground Teams Successfully Troubleshoot Orion’s Toilet  NASA (.gov)

item.title: Artemis II astronauts find hidden Easter eggs as they close in on the moon - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Artemis II astronauts find hidden Easter eggs as they close in on the moon  Yahoo

  4. Artemis II crew celebrates Easter. See their message to Earth.  Yahoo

item.title: The powerful new Rubin Observatory just found 11,000 new asteroids and measured 'tens of thousands more' - Space
item.description:
1. The powerful new Rubin Observatory just found 11,000 new asteroids and measured 'tens of thousands more'  Space
  2. The Rubin Observatory just turned the night sky into a live feed  Federal News Network
  3. 11,000 Asteroids Have Just Been Found — 33 Are Near-Earth Objects  Forbes
  4. The Vera C. Rubin Observatory Is About to Revolutionize Astronomy  ZME Science
  5. 11,000 new asteroids! Astronomers call it ‘tip of iceberg’  EarthSky

item.title: Satellite mirror plans could disrupt sleep and ecosystems worldwide, scientists say - The Guardian
item.description:

item.title: Chance of alien life ‘goes to heart’ of space missions, Nasa chief says - The Guardian
item.description:

item.title: Newly discovered fossils give scientists first look at the evolution of early complex animals - PBS
item.description:
1. Newly discovered fossils give scientists first look at the evolution of early complex animals  PBS

  3. Chinese fossils reveal a primordial burst of animal evolution  Reuters
  4. Humans' closest invertebrate ancestors date back much further than thought  Phys.org
  5. ‘Jaw-dropping’ fossils reset the clock on when complex animals evolved  Scientific American

item.title: Bennu sample reveals how water flowed through the newly forming asteroid - Phys.org
item.description:

item.title: They Went Snorkeling in a Cave and Found the Floor Covered in Ancient Bones - Indian Defence Review
item.description:

item.title: Exploding primordial black holes might have reshaped the early universe, and created all matter as we know it - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Exploding primordial black holes might have reshaped the early universe, and created all matter as we know it  Phys.org
  2. LIGO May Have Detected The First Primordial Black Hole, Scientists Say  ScienceAlert
  3. Astrophysicists are close to proving the existence of primordial black holes  The Brighter Side of News
  4. Rare Gravitational Wave Discovery May Hold the Key to First Evidence of Primordial Black Holes  The Daily Galaxy
  5. Scientists Detect Mysterious High-Energy Neutrino, Sparking Theories of Exploding Black Hole  National Today
